```md
## Goal
- Preserve selective-memory details for multiple incident topics and exact identifiers.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Preserve exact strings for future selective-memory questions.
- Avoid stale neighboring details listed per topic.

## Progress
### Done
- Captured incident details for checkout idempotency cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for search index freshness cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for email digest batching cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for webhook replay protection cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for cache namespace TTL cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for feature rollout sampling cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for csv import quarantine cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for notification fanout cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for session rotation cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for audit log redaction cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for report export leases cases 1 and 2.
- Captured incident details for tenant config snapshots case 1.

### In Progress
- (none)

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- checkout idempotency: accepted per-cart idempotency key store; rejected global checkout mutex because shard-local ordering is preserved while unrelated carts retry independently.
- search index freshness: accepted epoch-stamped publish barrier; rejected blind last-write-wins publish because slow crawls cannot replace newer snapshots.
- email digest batching: accepted workspace-local send window; rejected single UTC midnight cron because quiet-hour promises are kept without one global clock.
- webhook replay protection: accepted nonce ledger keyed by provider event id; rejected timestamp-only replay window because fast replays are blocked inside timestamp tolerance.
- cache namespace TTL: accepted namespace maximum TTL cap; rejected provider-wide hard-coded TTL because bounded freshness budgets stay namespace-specific.
- feature rollout sampling: accepted stable hash over actor and flag; rejected per-request random sampling because user buckets stay stable across refreshes.
- csv import quarantine: accepted row-level quarantine ledger; rejected abort entire import on first invalid row because clean rows can commit with diagnostics for rejected rows.
- notification fanout: accepted per-channel bounded queue; rejected unbounded global fanout array because slow SMS cannot exhaust memory for email/push.
- session rotation: accepted one-use previous-cookie grace; rejected accepting every old cookie until expiry because logout races are avoided while replay opportunities close.
- audit log redaction: accepted field allowlist redactor; rejected regex-only secret scrubber because new sensitive fields remain private before regex updates.
- report export leases: accepted expiring worker lease with compare-and-swap; rejected permanent worker ownership because abandoned exports can recover without duplicating active work.
- tenant config snapshots: accepted request-scoped config snapshot; rejected live config reads at every call site because one request stays internally consistent while later requests can see updates.

## Next Steps
- (none)
```
